OVERVIEW
THE SITUATION

Overview.

Silicon Data has done what most data companies spend years attempting: it built the benchmark before the market knew it needed one. Carmen Li, a former Bloomberg data executive and Ph.D. in Mathematical Physics, founded the company on a simple premise — GPU compute is becoming a traded commodity, and traded commodities require transparent pricing. The Silicon Data H100 Rental Index (BBG: SDH100RT), now live on Bloomberg and Refinitiv terminals, is the first of its kind. It covers 50+ providers across 50 countries, drawing on 3.5 million data points.

Revenue scaled 5x between January and April 2026 with no formal sales infrastructure. DRW and Jump Trading — firms that recognize data-as-infrastructure businesses — are backers. The product is real, the market is moving, and the window to own the category narrative is open right now.

Title TK's role: convert that market position into media authority. We build the signal-to-story machine that makes Silicon Data the first call for any journalist, analyst, or CFO trying to understand what AI costs.

NARRATIVE ARCHITECTURE

Themes and
storylines.

Four narrative pillars that position SiliconData as the independent measurement standard for GPU compute pricing.

01
Trust: Measurement credibility requires independence. SiliconData operates as a neutral third party with no stake in GPU sales or cloud platform revenue. Funded by compute buyers who need transparency, the company's incentive structure aligns with accurate reporting rather than vendor promotion. This positioning mirrors Bloomberg's independence in financial markets — data integrity over distribution relationships.
02
Comparability: Enterprise GPU buyers allocate budgets across hyperscalers, neoclouds, and spot markets without standardized benchmarks. SiliconData's normalized pricing methodology enables apples-to-apples comparison across providers, geographies, and lease structures. When a CFO evaluates a $10M compute commitment, comparable data transforms guesswork into informed capital allocation.
03
Adaptability: As compute liquidity grows, markets evolve — new rental structures emerge, pricing mechanisms shift, regional supply dynamics change. SiliconData's infrastructure is built to scale with market complexity. The platform tracks 50+ providers across 50 countries, capturing pricing variations as they happen. When the market invents new trading instruments or pricing models, the measurement layer adapts in real time.
04
Category Leadership: Every mature market crowns a measurement standard. GPU compute is reaching that inflection point. The company that establishes methodological rigor and reporter trust first becomes the default citation — the S&P of AI infrastructure. Speed determines who owns the category.
THE PROGRAM

Program.

SiliconData has a credible point of view, proprietary data and insights, and a moment in the market that makes it timely and highly relevant. The program operates on the Nielsen model for data storytelling: quick-turn pitching based on market signals captured in real time. When B200 prices spike, when H100 spot rates soften, when hyperscaler CapEx guidance shifts — we move from data to story in hours, not days.

PHASE 1

Foundation

Build the narrative infrastructure and trust positioning.

  • Data audit: Map SiliconData's existing content corpus (weekly blogs, market reports, pricing indices)
  • Methodology storytelling: Build trust narrative around normalization framework, breadth of coverage, independence positioning
  • Category narrative architecture: Establish "GPU compute's S&P" positioning across founder, company, and category storylines
  • Spokesperson preparation: CEO media training for tier-one financial interviews
  • Proof library assembly: Verified pricing records, coverage breadth stats, methodology transparency materials
PHASE 2

Infrastructure

Build the signal-to-story machine.

  • Signal-to-story system: Build the machine that converts dashboard data into pitch-ready narratives
  • Media target map: Tier-one financial reporters, semiconductor analysts, cloud infrastructure beat
  • Press materials foundation: Boilerplate language, exec bios, data visualization standards
  • Reporter relationship mapping: Identify who covers hyperscaler CapEx, GPU allocation, data center economics
  • Embargo protocol: Structure for exclusive data releases to tier-one outlets
PHASE 3

Velocity

Activate weekly data storytelling cadence.

  • Weekly data storytelling cadence: Active pitching tied to market movements
  • Tier-one financial media activation: WSJ, Bloomberg, FT, Reuters, CNBC placements
  • Analyst briefing program: Gartner, IDC, Forrester introductions
  • Thought leadership pipeline: CEO bylines, podcast interviews, conference panel submissions
  • Momentum measurement: Track coverage, analyst citations, client inbound attribution

NORTH TK NARRATIVE INTELLIGENCE

From signal
to story.

North TK is our proprietary platform that transforms raw market signals into media-ready narratives. Built specifically for data-driven companies that move fast, it compresses the signal-to-story timeline from days to hours.

SIGNALS PIPELINE

Capture what matters

Every data point SiliconData tracks becomes a potential story. North TK ingests signals — GPU price spikes, H100 spot pricing shifts, hyperscaler CapEx guidance changes — and surfaces the ones worth pitching.

SIGNAL INTELLIGENCE

From data to narrative in one view

Each signal becomes a structured brief with verbatim capture, analyst notes, index context, and traceability. The system shows exactly where the story came from and where it flows to next.

STORYLINE SYNTHESIS

Themes stitched from signals

Storylines connect multiple signals into coherent narratives. One pricing anomaly is a data point. Three related signals across different markets become "The GPU Economy's Three Inflection Points" — a tier-one pitch angle.

CAMPAIGN ORCHESTRATION

Storylines become artifacts

Campaigns turn storylines into shippable content — press releases, media pitches, blog posts, bylines. Every claim is traceable back to the original signal. Every output is provenanced.

THE RESULT

When H100 spot pricing spikes 18% week-over-week on May 7, 2026, North TK captures the signal, contextualizes it against historical ranges, connects it to enterprise inference demand, and generates a pitch-ready brief — all before lunch. That's the infrastructure advantage.

BOLD IDEAS

Bold ideas.

Creative activations that position Silicon Data as the market standard and Carmen Li as the authoritative voice on compute economics.

FLAGSHIP ACTIVATION

The Compute Ticker

Put the price of intelligence on the wall.

Every major asset class has a ticker that became cultural shorthand: the Dow. WTI crude. The VIX. GPU compute — the raw material of the intelligence economy — doesn't have one yet. Silicon Data owns that moment.

The centerpiece activation: a physical Compute Ticker — a large-format, stock-ticker-style display installed in a high-visibility location in Manhattan's financial district (Nasdaq MarketSite, One World Trade lobby, or a leased Times Square digital face during a launch week). It runs the Silicon Data GPU Index live. When H100s spike, the ticker moves. When B200 pricing tightens ahead of Nvidia earnings, it shows. The visual becomes the press photo for every story.

Also offer an embeddable web ticker that any media outlet, hedge fund, or tech company can drop into their site — persistent brand presence everywhere AI infrastructure is discussed. The ticker becomes the standard citation across financial media.

NEWS HIJACKING

Breaking News Response Desk

Nvidia announces. A hyperscaler misses CapEx guidance. Silicon Data is the first authoritative voice with actual market data — within hours. Pre-build 12 "fill-in-the-data" response templates for predictable triggers.

Sample: "Nvidia just announced Blackwell Ultra. Here's What the GPU Market Did in the Next 90 Minutes."

Outlets: CNBC, Reuters, Bloomberg TV, The Information

QUARTERLY REPORT

The Compute Quarterly

The CPI for AI infrastructure. A quarterly embargoed report tracking GPU price movements, regional cost differentials, forward curve signals, and the "Compute Misery Index." Becomes what journalists cite when writing about AI cost trends.

Sample: "The cost of training a frontier model fell 18% last quarter — but East Coast infrastructure premiums widened."

Outlets: WSJ, FT, Forbes, Fortune, Gartner, IDC

GEOGRAPHY ANGLE

Wall Street Data, Silicon Valley Subject

AI is happening in SF. The data infrastructure tracking AI is being built in NY by a Bloomberg alum. The financial rigor of Wall Street — independent indexes, structured methodology — applied to the wild west of AI compute.

Sample: "While AI startups cluster in SF, the woman tracking what it costs built her company the way Bloomberg was built — in New York."

Outlets: New York Times, Crain's NY, NY Magazine

FOUNDER PROFILE

Carmen Li: The Woman Who Priced the Future

Three narratives converge: female founder in infrastructure AI, Bloomberg alum applying financial logic to AI, Ph.D. physicist creating the first GPU benchmark. Targets profiles, podcasts, keynotes, Women in Finance features.

Sample: "She helped build Bloomberg's data infrastructure. Then she left to do the same for GPU pricing."

Outlets: Bloomberg Businessweek, FT, Fortune, The Information

MACRO NARRATIVE

Compute as the New Oil

When East Coast H100 prices diverge from West Coast by 18%, that's a structural signal. AI infrastructure is becoming a geopolitical asset. Silicon Data's Sovereign Map tracks these flows as they happen.

Sample: "GPU rental prices in Malaysia are now 35% cheaper than Northern Virginia. Here's what that means for AI capacity."

Outlets: FT, Reuters, TIME, The Economist

RECURRING DATA DROP

The Hyperscaler Efficiency Scorecard

Hold CapEx announcements accountable. Contextualizes every hyperscaler announcement with index data. Recurring initiative timed to earnings season — Carmen Li as the market commentator quoted in every story.

Sample: "Microsoft added $14B in compute capacity. Silicon Data's index shows customers paid a 15% scarcity premium all quarter anyway."

Outlets: WSJ, Bloomberg, CNBC, Barron's

CONTENT ENGINE

Forbes Council Column by Carmen Li

Monthly column under Carmen Li's byline targeting C-suite and finance readers. Framing: "the CFO's guide to the AI compute economy." Each column leads with a Silicon Data index data point and contextualizes it for enterprise budget decisions. Builds SEO, establishes Carmen as a recurring voice for enterprise finance audiences, creates a pipeline of analyst relationships.

Phase 1: Forbes Council application
Phase 2: First Forbes Council draft
Phase 3: Forbes Council publishes; ongoing
THE TEAM

Team.

Title TK's founders built their careers leading agencies LaunchSquad, Sparkpr, and Codeword. We are active day-to-day leading strategy and execution.

Co-Founder

Jason Throckmorton

Jason has spent his career helping visionary teams turn bold ideas into market-leading businesses. From co-founding a global PR agency (LaunchSquad) to working as the first head of marketing at Scope3, he has worked at the intersection of communications, content, and strategy, partnering with tech companies to develop marketing strategies, shape stories, and build momentum. He has been an advisor, investor, early team member, and strategic partner to many companies that lead their industries and have created billions in value.

LinkedIn →
Co-Founder

Cristina Pena

Cristina has worked in AI since 2014, supporting early work with MindMeld through its acquisition by Cisco, and continuing on to recent AI companies including Stability AI and Lightning AI. She focuses on client relations and ensuring programs stay strategically aligned, with clear intent behind every initiative and a direct connection to business goals. She also enjoys the chase of media relations, shaping stories and securing coverage across a variety of top-tier and emerging outlets.

LinkedIn →
About Title TK

Title TK is a strategic communications firm for growth-stage technology companies. Small, senior, and operator-led, we embed with clients rather than service them from a distance. Our work drives three outcomes: Recognition (building awareness in your market), Relevance (owning your category), and Revenue (growing the business through narrative that performs). We help clients succeed in any climate by putting the entrepreneur first and grounding solutions in results.

Title TK was founded by Cristina Pena (co-led the emerging tech practice and launched and led the B2B2C practice at Spark), Jason Throckmorton (co-founded the award-winning marketing and communications agency LaunchSquad), and Mike Barash (founded and led Codeword through its acquisition by Waggener Edstrom).

Prepared by Title TK
